title: Bumble Q2 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: Bumble delivered strong Q2 results with total revenue of $260 million, representing 18% year-over-year growth. The company exceeded expectations with accelerating paying users and robust profitability. Total paying users: 3.6 million (up 20% YoY). Bumble app revenue: $208 million (up 23%). Adjusted EBITDA: $67 million (26% margin). Free cash flow: $40 million in Q2. Whitney Wolfe Herd highlighted several significant product developments: Compliments Feature is gaining traction, with recipients being 70% more likely to get a match, driving improved brand perception. Best Bees AI: The new AI-powered recommendation feature was bundled into Bumble Premium in May and is showing positive early indicators, boosting both new subscribers and renewals. Subscription Expansion: Management is testing both lower-priced tiers for Gen Z and premium higher-priced offerings for users seeking more curated experiences. Bumble announced two major initiatives: Official Acquisition - Acquired the app that helps couples strengthen existing relationships through date ideas and mood check-ins. Bumble For Friends Launch - A standalone friendship app now live in multiple countries, showing strong Gen Z engagement metrics. Badoo posted its first year-over-year revenue growth since H1 2021, with sequential paying user additions of 34,000. 2023 Outlook (Narrowed): Full-year revenue $1.055-$1.072 billion (17-19% growth). Bumble app revenue $852-$863 million (23-24% growth). Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion at least 100 basis points YoY.
